label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out if following those instructions brings me back to the starting point. I need to keep track of my direction and position as I go through each step. Let me start by visualizing this. First, the instructions are: Turn right. Take 9 steps. Take 10 steps. Turn left. Take 10 steps. Turn right. Turn around. Take 4 steps. Let me break it down step by step. Starting point: Assume I'm facing North. I haven't moved yet, so my position is (0,0) and direction is North. 1. Turn right. So from North, a right turn would make me face East. 2. Take 9 steps. Moving East, so my position becomes (9,0). 3. Take 10 steps. Still moving East, so I add 10 more steps. New position is (9+10, 0) = (19, 0). 4. Turn left. Facing East, a left turn would make me face North. 5. Take 10 steps. Now moving North from (19,0) to (19, 0+10) = (19, 10). 6. Turn right. Facing North, a right turn would make me face East. 7. Turn around. Turning around from East would make me face West. 8. Take 4 steps. Now moving West, so from (19, 10) moving West 4 steps: 19 - 4 = 15. So new position is (15, 10). So my final position is (15,10), and I'm facing West. The starting point was (0,0), so I haven't returned to the starting point. So the answer should be No. Wait, but let me check again to make sure I didn't make a mistake in the direction changes. Starting direction: North 1. Turn right → East 2. Move 9 steps
